q: Earth's atmosphere and clouds absorb or scatter as much as $54 \%$ of the incoming sunlight.
The sunlight that reaches the ground consists of visible light, infrared, and ultraviolet radiation.
Irradiance is measured in $W \cdot m^{-2}$ or $kW \cdot m^{-2}$.
Insolation is the solar irradiance received on a horizontal surface such as ground.
The solar constant has an intensity of approximately $1350 W \cdot m^{-2}$ above the earth's atmosphere.
On a clear day at solar noon, solar radiation has an intensity of approximately $1000 W \cdot m^{-2}$ at the earth's surface.
The three main ways to harness solar energy are photovoltaic, solar heating, and concentrated solar power.
A solar cell is basically a p-n junction diode.
Power conversion efficiency is the ratio of solar power received to the electrical power delivered.
Solar heating systems in homes include a solar collector, insulated piping, and a hot water storage tank.
Flat plate collectors use copper pipes attached to an absorber plate.
Concentrated solar power systems use mirrors or lenses to concentrate sunlight.
The output of a wind turbine is proportional to the cube of the wind speed.
Wind power potential increases by a factor of eight when wind speed doubles.
Hydropower is one of the oldest and largest sources of renewable energy.
A penstock is an open canal that regulates water flow with gates and valves.
In a pumped storage facility, energy is stored by pumping water from a lower reservoir to an upper reservoir.
Tidal energy is produced by the gravitational pull of the moon on the spinning earth.
Ocean thermal-energy conversion plants exploit the temperature difference between surface and deep water.
Geothermal energy is taken from the Earth's core.
Biomass is renewable organic material that comes from plants and animals.
Direct combustion is the most common method for converting biomass to useful energy.
Biomass gasification produces synthesis gas, also known as syngas.
The biological conversion of biomass into ethanol involves fermentation.
Biogas or biomethane is produced in anaerobic digesters.
